title: Serverless Email Sender with Amazon Simple-Email-Service

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your Username and Password in the lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
Once Signed in to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.?
Navigate to Amazon Simple Email Service in the AWS Console and click on Identities in the left-side of the console.
Task 2: Verify SES Email Identities
Click Create Identity and then select Email Address
Add one sender email address (e.g., email.sender@gmail.com) and Click on Create Identity,
Task 2: Verify SES Email Identities
Again, We are going to create another Identity, So Go to Identities and Select Create Identity.
Select Email Address and give another email address (e,g., email.recipient@gmail.com) here. Click Create Identities.
Open your inbox in both the account and click the verification link from AWS.
You can check from the Identities tab if the email ID's are verified.
Task 2: Verify SES Email Identities

tasks.title: Task 3: Create and Configure S3 Bucket

steps.text: Navigate to the S3 console, Select Create Bucket
Bucket Name: email-whiz-bucket.
Task 3: Create and Configure S3 Bucket
Uncheck Block all public access and Check the acknowledge button.
Task 3: Create and Configure S3 Bucket
Click Create Bucket.
Go to the bucket we created and select Upload.
Click on Add Files, Upload email.html and Click on Upload.
Click on the uploaded object and Click Open button to test whether the object is working.
Task 4: Upload and Configure HTML
You can see the html static page hosted through S3.
Task 4: Upload and Configure HTML
Navigate to Lambda Function and click on Create Function
Choose Author from scratch
Function name: emailsendingfunction
Runtime: Python 3.12
In the Change default execution role section, Click on Use an existing function, Select SES_lambda
Click Create Function
Task 5: Create the Lambda Function
In the Lambda function which we created now, Click on the Code Source and replace the following function code.
Be sure to replace 'your_verified_sender@example.com' with the actual verified sender from SES.
Save the code by Clicking Deploy.
Task 6: Lambda Function Code

tasks.title: Task 7: Run and Test Lambda Manually

steps.text: Click Test tab from the Lambda Console.
Select Create new event.
Name the event name as test_email_event and click Save.
JSON Format:
Be sure to replace the recipient email address and Click Save.
Click Test to invoke the function.
Check the function execution for logs.
Task 7: Run and Test Lambda Manually
Navigate to API Gateway and Click on Create an API.
Select HTTP API and Click on Build.
Task 8: Create API Gateway
Set API name: email-api
Under Integration, Select Lambda, and Select the Lambda function which we created earlier
Click on Next.
Task 8: Create API Gateway
In the Configure Routes page, Click on Add Routes.
Select method as POST
Replace Resource Path as /send-email
In the Integration Target, confirm your Lambda function is selected.
Click on Next.
Task 8: Create API Gateway
In the Define stages page, Set the Stage name to prod
Turn on the Auto-Deploy toggle.
Click on Next.
Task 8: Create API Gateway
Review and Click Create.
Task 8: Create API Gateway

tasks.title: Task 9: Add Integration and Deploy API Gateway

steps.text: In the API Gateway panel, Select Routes option in the left side of the console.
You will see the /send-email listed under POST method.
Task 9: Add Integration and Deploy API Gateway
Same in the left side of the panel, Choose CORS, Click on Configure.
Set the following Values,
Access-Control-Allow-Origin : *
Access-Control-Allow-Headers : content-type
Access-Control-Allow-Methods : POST
Click Save Changes after entering the above values.
Task 9: Add Integration and Deploy API Gateway
Click on APIs in the left side of the console
In the left side of the console, You will see a API Link, Click on it
Task 10 : Get your API Endpoints
Copy the invoke URL.
Task 10 : Get your API Endpoints
In the Browser, We opened earlier through S3, Paste the URL into API Gateway URL and add /send-email in the end.
In the Recipient Email, Add your recipient email you given before that is verified in SES.
Add any Message, in the message box, you can also enter HTML tags to make highlight your mail.
Click Send Email
You will receive Email Sent! message!
Task 10 : Get your API Endpoints

tasks.title: Task 11: Check in Inbox for the Message

steps.text: Open the recipient's email. Navigate to Inbox.
Check Inbox or Spam for the mail function.
You can find the message you sent through the browser in the email.
Task 11: Check in Inbox for the Message
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and show you whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Task 12: Validation of the Lab
You have successfully logged into AWS portal
You have successfully Verified Email identities in Simple Email service
You have successfully uploaded a static HTML form to an S3 bucket.
You have successfully created a Lambda function
You have successfully created API gateway and enabled CORS for browser based requests.
You have successfully send the mail to recipients
Sign out of the AWS Console.
Confirm that resources are working as expected or clean them up if needed.
You have completed the Serverless Email Marketing lab.
